reasons: Angela can protect Argus before or after his all-in window, helping him keep pressure in melee range.
Argus benefits from added shielding and sustain while forcing enemies to answer his dive.

proof.summary: Argus creates melee pressure, while Angela adds protection and sustain to extend his all-in window.
worksBestWhen: Angela attaches before Argus is forced to use his ultimate.
Argus commits while enemy burst is already drawn out.
failureCases: Argus uses his ultimate too late.
Angela is unavailable during the side-lane fight.
The enemy kites Argus without fully committing damage.

reasons: Floryn can support Argus even when he pressures a side area away from the main team.
Argus benefits from global sustain when he commits to a fight and needs to survive after forcing enemy cooldowns.

proof.summary: Argus pressures away from the team, while Floryn can still contribute sustain during his commit window.
worksBestWhen: Argus fights when he can hit targets during his survival window.
failureCases: Argus is crowd-controlled and cannot deal damage.
Floryn's key support skill is unavailable.
Argus overextends after the sustain window ends.

reasons: Rafaela can help Argus chase targets or disengage after committing.
Argus benefits from movement support because he needs to stay in melee range to keep pressure.

proof.summary: Argus needs to stay in melee range, while Rafaela helps him keep contact or escape after the fight.
worksBestWhen: Rafaela supports Argus before enemies fully kite him.
Argus enters after enemy dash skills are spent.
failureCases: Argus is kited outside basic-attack range.
Rafaela is too far from the side fight.
Enemy burst waits until Argus's survival window ends.

reasons: Mathilda can help Argus enter or exit fights with shield and mobility support.
Argus benefits from extra approach tools because he can be kited before reaching priority targets.

proof.summary: Argus supplies the melee threat, while Mathilda helps solve his approach and escape problem.
worksBestWhen: Mathilda positions ahead of Argus's engage path.
Argus saves his catch tool for confirmed follow-up.
failureCases: Argus takes the mobility path into layered crowd control.
Mathilda is forced away before Argus commits.
Enemy disengage tools are still available.

reasons: Diggie can protect Argus from normal crowd-control chains while Argus needs uninterrupted melee uptime.
Argus benefits when Diggie keeps him free to attack during his survival and healing window.

proof.summary: Argus wants uninterrupted melee uptime, while Diggie can reduce crowd-control disruption for nearby teammates.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves Time Journey for the real enemy control chain.
Argus is close enough to receive the protection.
failureCases: Diggie uses ultimate before Argus commits.
